name: Captain's Cabin, B-59
description: A compartment barely large enough for a bunk, a fold-down desk, and a wall-mounted safe — the only lockable private space on the boat. The safe door is ajar. Papers are spread across the desk in a disorder that is itself a form of evidence: Savitsky has been working in here, alone, for a long time.
atmosphericDetails: The fold-down desk holds a handwritten log that is not the official ship's log — personal notes in a cramped, deteriorating script that grows less legible toward the bottom of the page.
A half-eaten tin of preserved fish sits beside the log, the lid bent back — a man who ate alone and did not notice the taste.
The safe contains the standing orders document, a sealed envelope with a Moscow frank, and a photograph of a young man in an air force uniform.
